questionText: A stone is thrown so that, $t$ seconds after release, its position in metres is modelled by $x = 8t$ and $y = 20t - 5t^{2}$. Find the position of the stone when $t = 3$.

steps.explanation: To find where the stone is, we simply substitute the given time into each position equation. No differentiation is needed because we want position, not velocity.
Putting $t = 3$ into the $x$-equation gives the horizontal distance travelled.
The stone is $24$ metres from its launch point horizontally.
Putting the same time into the $y$-equation gives the height of the stone.
Working out each term and subtracting gives the height at that instant.
So after $3$ seconds the stone is at the point $(24,\,15)$, measured in metres from its starting point.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: When $t = 3$ s the stone is at the point $(24,\,15)$ metres.
